media.altText: Lashi Alpine Wetland with waterbirds and mountain backdrop

sections.subtitle: Rare birds, wetland conservationists, real rescues
sections.text: Pickup from your Lijiang hotel and transfer 30 minutes to Lashi Alpine Wetland. We start with the Naxi worldview that protected this landscape, tour the bird museum, then walk a wetland survey with binoculars. Then on to the wildlife rescue station to meet local conservationists, and a quiet final walk through the most pristine section of the reserve. You'll leave knowing new bird species and how they are protected.

items.title: What's Special
items.subtitle: Wetland birding with binoculars
items.highlight: A guided wetland walk with hands-on bird survey and a behind-the-scenes look at local conservation work

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Half-day
items.highlight: Morning: 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM | Afternoon: 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM

items.title: Location
items.subtitle: Alpine Wetland
items.highlight: Lashi Plateau Wetland Nature Reserve | Altitude 2,440m

badge.label: Wetlands, Birds, and the People Who Protect Them

items.title: Naxi Beliefs about Nature
items.subtitle: Dongba Worldview
items.text: Learn how Naxi tradition views mountains, rivers, and animals as relatives — a worldview that has shaped wetland protection in this valley for centuries.

image.altText: Naxi Dongba cultural symbols related to nature

items.title: Visit Lashi Wetland Park
items.subtitle: Boardwalk Trail
items.text: Wander the easy lakeside boardwalk, watching for herons, grebes, and the occasional crane feeding in the shallows.

items.title: Visit Local Bird Museum
items.subtitle: Species Briefing
items.text: Tour the small but well-curated wetland museum to identify the species you're likely to spot — from resident swamphens to wintering bar-headed geese.

items.title: Bird Biodiversity Survey Activity
items.subtitle: Field Survey
items.text: Join a guided survey with binoculars and field cards, recording species counts that feed directly into the reserve's conservation database.

image.altText: Binoculars and bird field card during wetland survey

items.title: Visit Wildlife Rescue Station
items.subtitle: Rehab in Action
items.text: Meet the team rehabilitating injured waterbirds and learn what it takes to return a black stork or a black-necked crane back to the wild.

image.altText: Wildlife rescue station caring for an injured waterbird

items.title: Meet Local Conservationists
items.subtitle: Reserve Staff
items.text: Meet the reserve staff and hear their daily work: patrolling wetlands, monitoring nests, and negotiating with farmers to protect bird habitat.

image.altText: Local wetland conservationist explaining reserve work

items.title: Learn about Conservation Efforts
items.subtitle: Practical Challenges
items.text: Discover the practical challenges of wetland protection in Yunnan — from invasive species control to balancing sustainable tourism with fragile habitat.

image.altText: Wetland conservation efforts in the Lashi valley

items.title: Learn about Local Plants & Insects
items.subtitle: The Small Life
items.text: Look closer at the wetland's smaller life: native aquatic plants, dragonflies, and the insects that form the base of the bird food chain.

image.altText: Wetland plants and insects along the Lashi boardwalk

items.title: Walk in Wetland Nature Reserve
items.subtitle: Pristine Section
items.text: End your tour with a quiet walk through the most pristine section of the reserve, where the bird song and the wind across the water are all you'll hear.

image.altText: Quiet walking path through the Lashi wetland nature reserve

activities: Meet your guide at your Lijiang hotel and transfer 30 minutes to Lashi Wetland Park, where you'll start with an introduction to Naxi beliefs about nature and the wetland's ecological role. Tour the bird museum to learn the local species, then walk into the wetland on a guided biodiversity survey — binoculars and field cards in hand, recording the waterbirds you spot. Continue to the wildlife rescue station and conservation bureau, where you'll meet the staff, learn about wetland protection efforts, and hear stories of birds returned to the wild. Finish with a quiet walk in the most pristine section of the nature reserve, spotting endemic plants and more birds, before driving back to your Lijiang hotel.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: English-Speaking Coordinator/Guide (Lynne Lyu and/or Lukasz Madrzynski), Entrance Fees to Lashi Wetland Park & Bird Museum, Binoculars and Bird Field Cards, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: Personal Expenses (Souvenirs, etc.), Voluntary Gratuities for the Guide
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 9:00 AM (morning tour) or 2:00 PM (afternoon tour) at your Lijiang hotel | Return: Approximately 1:00 PM or 6:00 PM to your Lijiang hotel

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: Which season is the best for bird watching?
items.answer: There are many bird species that can be seen throughout the year, but the best time is between November and March when great numbers of migratory birds fly in to spend winter here. Resident species like swamphens, egrets, and grebes among others are visible year-round.

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: Comfortable walking shoes, layers for changing temperatures (the wetland is cooler and more windy than Lijiang Old Town), sun protection, umbrella and/or raincoat, a water bottle, and your camera. We provide binoculars and field cards.

items.question: Is this suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes! This tour is family-friendly. Children under 12 should be accompanied by an adult. The 4-5 km walk is at a slow pace with frequent stops, achievable for most ages. The bird survey in particular is a hit with kids who enjoy looking for birds and using binoculars.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 7+ days before the tour. 50% refund for 3-6 days. No refund within 48 hours.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: The tour runs in light rain (remember to bring umbrellas and/or raincoats). For heavy storms we'll shorten the outdoor boardwalk portions and spend more time at the bird museum and conservation bureau.

items.question: Are there any physical requirements?
items.answer: Basic mobility is enough. You'll walk 4-5 km on flat wetland boardwalks and gentle paths at a slow pace, with frequent stops for birdwatching and storytelling. Suitable for most fitness levels.

items.question: How do I get to the meeting point?
items.answer: We provide pickup from any hotel in Lijiang Old Town or the surrounding area. If you're staying outside Lijiang, please contact us to arrange an alternative meeting point.

items.question: What language do the guides speak?
items.answer: We are fluent in English, Polish and Chinese.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation and sustainable development?
items.answer: Each booking directly supports the Lashi Wetland Park. Your survey counts feed the reserve's biodiversity database. Our small-group format (max 6) minimizes impact on this fragile wetland while maximizing the benefit to the people protecting it.

seo.metaTitle: Lashi Wetland Birding Tour | Yunnan Half-Day Ecotour from Lijiang
seo.metaDescription: Half-day Lashi Wetland birding ecotour from Lijiang, Yunnan, China. Walk with local conservationists through alpine wetland, spot Black-necked Cranes (winter) and migratory shorebirds. Small-group Yunnan eco tour.
